news.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran, Hezbollah isn't over after Tehran says it's halting strikes
news.summary: The U.S. and Iran agreed to a fragile ceasefire in mid-April. Iran and Israel traded strikes Sunday night for the first time since the agreement.

news.title: Food supply 'not at risk' after new Texas screwworm cases, USDA secretary says
news.summary: The U.S. will lean on the same playbook it did starting in the late 1950s. Part of that involves releasing sterile insects to suppress the pest's population.

news.title: Chip rebound has one trader buying protection
news.summary: Chip stocks are bounding back Monday, but the rebound doesn't mean tech investors are in the clear, according to Mike Khouw.

news.title: Household worries over finances hit highest level since July 2022, New York Fed survey shows
news.summary: The central bank's monthly survey showed the inflation outlook mostly unchanged. But the general perception of conditions deteriorated, the survey found. The inflation outlook was mostly unchanged, the central bank said.

news.title: Weight loss drug maker sinks 23% after new safety data spooks investors
news.summary: Analysts concerned over commercial opportunity due to side effects. While the experimental drug met its key targets, analysts are concerned over the commercial opportunity.

news.title: We thought we found the perfect luxury retirement community, but it’s millions of dollars in debt. Are we trapped?
news.summary: “If we were to leave, we would lose a portion of our buy-in — about $80,000,” he says.

news.title: Intel’s stock soars as the company’s ‘blue-chip roster’ of customers looks to be growing
news.summary: Investors are cheering the idea that Intel could serve as a backup chip manufacturer for Nvidia and Google. Intel is the world's second-largest chipmaker.

news.title: Millions of grandparents are spending their retirements raising their grandkids — and it’s taking a financial toll
news.summary: Grandparent-headed households are more likely to live in poverty. Grandparents head more than half of all households in the U.S., according to a new study.

news.title: Social Security is looking at a $500-a-month cut. Could a new bipartisan commission make a difference?
news.summary: A new legislative proposal would create a bipartisan commission to strengthen the finances of Social Security and Medicare. The proposal comes at a time when the programs are under pressure.

news.title: Will you be audited by Trump’s IRS? New data says there’s one group that can breathe a lot easier.
news.summary: A new report examines IRS audit activity at the start of the second Trump presidency. The report was published by the nonprofit watchdog group The Taxpayers Project. It was published to coincide with the second year of the Trump administration.
